audit_input_meta.converter_version: v2 (harness-driven, captures doubled L# sets and L4 Excel charts)
audit_input_meta.structural_note: Each row in the Lesson Hypothesis table is parsed via the harness parser (texture_lessons_harness/parser.js) which handles every cell shape seen in the doc: empty cells, single L1, sequential L1-Ln, doubled L1-L3 sets, L1- dash notation, inline run-ons like conclusionL2:, named Seminar entries, marker rows.

challenge.title: Why Big Dogs Get Passed Over
challenge.authentic_impact: Students will produce narrative profile cards for individual big dogs, written for the families walking the adoption kennels at St. Hubert's, by turning each dog's real history and observed behavior into a short, honest story that reframes the size that made adopters hesitate. The cards are printed and clipped to each dog's kennel. The cohort then briefs St. Hubert's, handing off the cards and explaining the thinking behind the campaign.

acts.title: Is This Even True?
acts.evidence_produced: Initial analysis of adoption data and a written claim about whether a real adoption gap exists.

acts.title: How a Wolf Became Both a Great Dane and a German Shepherd
acts.evidence_produced: Explanation of how domestication and selective breeding produced modern dog breeds.

acts.title: Meet Your Dog
acts.evidence_produced: A written assumption based on the dog's photo, a structured observation log, and a comparison of the two.

acts.title: Why Do People Pass Them By?
acts.core_question: Why do people pass them by?
acts.evidence_produced: Survey findings, evidence checks, and conclusions supported by data.

acts.title: Telling the Truth So It Moves Someone
acts.evidence_produced: Draft profile cards and reader feedback.

acts.title: The Cards Go Up
acts.core_question: What do we owe the shelter?
acts.evidence_produced: Final profile cards and partner briefing.

concept_days.concept_progression: Meet the long-stay dog; write a first prediction
Read and interpret the shelter's tables and bar graphs
concept_days.topic: Reading and interpreting tables and graphs
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Extract info from tables and graphs What information can they gather from the website

concept_days.concept_progression: Counts vs. rates; calculate an adoption rate
concept_days.topic: Raw counts vs. rates; unit rates

concept_days.concept_progression: Compare unequal groups by rate; spot the hidden factor
concept_days.topic: Comparing groups; misleading comparisons; hidden factors
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Compare fairly; name the hidden factor

concept_days.concept_progression: Write a data-backed claim (Act 1 evidence)
Same species? (Great Dane vs. Chihuahua) Write a first explanation to revise
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: A starting explanation of how a chihuahua and a great dane are the same animal

concept_days.concept_progression: Inheritance and variation
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Explain inheritance; identify variation L4 and L5 Same day

concept_days.concept_progression: Bred for a job (border collie reading)
concept_days.topic: Selective breeding; Explain the breeds (Act 2 evidence)
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Tie a breed trait to its original job

concept_days.concept_progression: Model a trait spreading over generations
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Model a trait becoming more common

concept_days.concept_progression: Dog Breed traits and role specialization
Write the photo assumption - What assumptions might people make about this dog?
Observation vs. inference (Horowitz aloud)
Meet the dog; keep an observation log
Assumption vs. reality (Act 3 evidence)
concept_days.topic: Comparing assumed vs. observed - How were first impressions right or wrong?

concept_days.concept_progression: Write a testable hypothesis
Design non-leading survey/interview questions
Gather, tally, and summarize responses
Test beliefs against facts (Act 4 evidence)
concept_days.topic: Supported vs. unsupported claims; testing explanations
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Evaluate evidence; spot overreach; revise a belief

concept_days.concept_progression: The "prove it" move: label (preconceptions) to evidence{Explore Facts using Data Base for dog bites and fatalities, cost using medication and food costs, restrictions on pet allowance in housing, size of dog vs. breed energy levels}
concept_days.topic: Labels (preconceptions) vs. specific details; the "prove it" move
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Replace labels with observed detail{“The things we're seeing in the survey - this is why they're not true or why this is true” The validity of the reasons}

concept_days.concept_progression: Claim only what you saw. Final analysis.
concept_days.topic: Claiming only what evidence supports; overclaiming
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Spot and fix overclaiming - grounding in evidence “What can you support with evidence?”

concept_days.concept_progression: What makes a story (Eddie + Billie mentor texts)
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Pick the strongest moment; structure a profile Take your position Study your audience Research both sides Structure your text Support your argument - Persuasive paragraph - write an opinion statement (topic), reasons (body paragraph), conclusion (reiterating opinion statement) , Add a conclusion

concept_days.concept_progression: Show, don't tell; sequence the profile
concept_days.topic: Showing vs. telling; sequencing and pacing
concept_days.evidence_drives_at: Show character in scenes; build the sequence

concept_days.concept_progression: Draft, test on a reader, revise (Act 5 evidence)
Finalize the card
Brief St. Hubert's (Act 6 evidence)
